chapter_title.en: Chapter 96 – Salāt of the one who fears an abhorrence

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/465)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/465)
text: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا هَالَهُ شَيْ‏ءٌ فَزِعَ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ وَ اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَ الصَّلاةِ.

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al-Fazl Bin ShAzaan , from Hammad Bin Isa, from Shuayb Al-Aqarquqy, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘It was so that whenever something dismayed Aliasws, heasws hastened to the Salāt, then recited this Verse [2:45] And seek Assistance through the patience and the Salāt’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Shu’ayb al-‘Aqarqufiy from abu Basir from abu ’Abd Allah, who has said the following: “If something dismayed Imam Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, he would feel restless for Salah (prayer). The Imam then read this, ’Seek help from exercising patience and performing Salah (prayer).’”
